# CWE-916 negative: salted iterative SHA-256 password storage (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-916-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-916 mitigated: random salt plus 100k SHA-256 iterations before storage.

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"encoding/hex"
	"net/http"
	"sync"
)

const hashIterations = 100_000

var accountsSafeMu sync.Mutex
var accountsSafe = map[string]string{}

func stretchPassword(password string, salt []byte) string {
	digest := append(salt, []byte(password)...)
	for i := 0; i < hashIterations; i++ {
		sum := sha256.Sum256(digest)
		digest = sum[:]
	}
	return hex.EncodeToString(salt) + ":" + hex.EncodeToString(digest)
}

func RegisterStrongHashPure() http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		username := r.FormValue("username")
		password := r.FormValue("password")
		salt := make([]byte, 16)
		if _, err := rand.Read(salt); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		stored := stretchPassword(password, salt)
		accountsSafeMu.Lock()
		accountsSafe[username] = stored
		accountsSafeMu.Unlock()
		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	}
}
